account.getBotBusinessConnection.summary: Bots may invoke this method to re-fetch the updateBotBusinessConnect constructor associated with a specific business connection_id , see here » for more info on connected business bots. This is needed for example for freshly logged in bots that are receiving some updateBotNewBusinessMessage , etc. updates because some users have already connected to the bot before it could login. In this case, the bot is receiving messages from the business connection, but it hasn't cached the associated updateBotBusinessConnect with info about the connection (can it reply to messages? etc.) yet, and cannot receive the old ones because they were sent when the bot wasn't logged into the session yet. This method can be used to fetch info about a not-yet-cached business connection, and should not be invoked if the info is already cached or to fetch changes, as eventual changes will automatically be sent as new updateBotBusinessConnect updates to the bot using the usual update delivery methods » .

params.limit: Number of bytes to be returned

upload.getCdnFileHashes.summary: Get SHA256 hashes for verifying downloaded CDN files

params.offset: Offset from which to start getting hashes

upload.getFileHashes.summary: Get SHA256 hashes for verifying downloaded files

params.offset: Offset from which to get file hashes

upload.reuploadCdnFile.summary: Request a reupload of a certain file to a CDN DC .

upload.saveBigFilePart.summary: Saves a part of a large file (over 10 MB in size) to be later passed to one of the methods.

params.fileId: Random file id, created by the client

upload.getWebFile.summary: Returns content of a web file, by proxying the request through telegram, see the webfile docs for more info . Note : the query must be sent to the DC specified in the webfile_dc_id MTProto configuration field .

params.offset: Number of bytes to be skipped
params.limit: Number of bytes to be returned

users.getFullUser.summary: Returns extended user info by ID.

upload.saveFilePart.summary: Saves a part of file for further sending to one of the methods.

params.fileId: Random file identifier created by the client
params.bytes: Binary data, content of a part

users.getRequirementsToContact.summary: Check whether we can write to the specified users, used to implement bulk checks for Premium-only messages » and paid messages » . For each input user, returns a RequirementToContact constructor (at the same offset in the vector) containing requirements to contact them.

users.getSavedMusic.summary: Get songs pinned to the user's profile, see here » for more info.

params.offset: Offset for pagination.
params.limit: Maximum number of results to return, see pagination
params.hash: Hash » of the IDs of previously added songs, to avoid returning any result if there was no change.

users.getSavedMusicByID.summary: Check if the passed songs are still pinned to the user's profile, or refresh the file references of songs pinned on a user's profile see here » for more info.

params.documents: The songs (here, file_reference can be empty to refresh file references).

users.getUsers.summary: Returns basic user info according to their identifiers.

users.setSecureValueErrors.summary: Notify the user that the sent passport data contains some errors The user will not be able to re-submit their Passport data to you until the errors are fixed (the contents of the field for which you returned the error must change). Use this if the data submitted by the user doesn't satisfy the standards your service requires for any reason. For example, if a birthday date seems invalid, a submitted document is blurry, a scan shows evidence of tampering, etc. Supply some details in the error message to make sure the user knows how to correct the issues.
